items.summary: Implement safeFetch utility function as global transport layer with base endpoint https://api.binance.com, 5000ms timeout using AbortController, 2 retry attempts with 500ms delay between retries, never throws errors (returns null on final failure), logs FETCH_RETRY event on each retry attempt and FETCH_FAIL event on final failure with {url, error} payload
Replace all existing Binance API fetch calls in binanceApi.ts with safeFetch utility, change base URL from https://data-api.binance.vision to https://api.binance.com for all endpoints

Replace fetchExchangeInfo and fetch24hrTickers functions with single fetchUniverse24h function that calls https://api.binance.com/api/v3/ticker/24hr using safeFetch, validates result is array, logs UNIVERSE_EMPTY event and returns empty array if result is not array or null, never throws errors

Modify universeFilter.ts to use new fetchUniverse24h function, lower quoteVolume filter threshold from 20,000,000 to 5,000,000, convert quoteVolume using Number(), implement fallback logic: if filtered array length is 0 then log UNIVERSE_FALLBACK event and take first 20 USDT-ending symbols from raw universe without volume filter
Add 300ms delay between per-symbol kline fetch calls only (not between other fetch types or entire engine loop), implement using setTimeout or Promise-based delay helper function

Modify engineLoop.ts to handle all safeFetch null returns gracefully: TOP5 refresh failures log TOP5_REFRESH_FAIL event but continue engine execution, kline fetch failures skip that symbol but continue processing other symbols, no fetch error may stop ENGINE_TICK or crash the engine, ensure zero unhandled promise rejections in engine loop

Add new log event types to store.ts LogEntry: FETCH_RETRY, FETCH_FAIL, UNIVERSE_EMPTY, UNIVERSE_FALLBACK, TOP5_REFRESH_FAIL, TOP5_SKIP_EMPTY, ensure all new events follow existing log structure with timestamp and payload

Update LogsTab.tsx to display new log event types (FETCH_RETRY, FETCH_FAIL, UNIVERSE_EMPTY, UNIVERSE_FALLBACK, TOP5_REFRESH_FAIL, TOP5_SKIP_EMPTY) with appropriate color coding: red for FETCH_FAIL and TOP5_REFRESH_FAIL, yellow for FETCH_RETRY and UNIVERSE_FALLBACK, default color for UNIVERSE_EMPTY and TOP5_SKIP_EMPTY
